metadata.summary: Bash deploy orchestrator for workspace/runtime separation: rsync source, manage virtualenvs, restart systemd services, and validate with health checks.

content.title: Deploy Scripts
content.summary: Bash-based deploy orchestrator for workspace/runtime separation: rsync source to runtime, manage Python virtualenvs with `pip install -e .`, restart systemd user services, and validate with a health check. A single `deploy.sh <service|all> [--rebuild-venv]` script handles the full lifecycle.

why.p: Ad-hoc deployment is error-prone and leaves no audit trail. A structured script enforces consistent deploy order (SDK before dependents), detects dependency changes, validates before and after, and provides a reproducible rollback path.

list.item: Adding or changing a service on a VPS using workspace/runtime separation pattern
Setting up initial deploy pipeline for a multi-service Python/Node platform
Post-deploy health validation before declaring success
Monorepos where workspace and runtime are the same directory

rule.statement: A deploy has five ordered phases: pre-checks → rsync → deps → restart → validate. Never skip pre-checks or post-validate in production.
rule.rationale: Skipping pre-checks lets deploys proceed with a missing env file or full disk, which leaves the runtime in a partial state. Skipping post-validation means a failed restart is not caught until a user hits an error.

rule.statement: Validate env file, disk space (>1 GB free), systemd user session, and runtime directory existence before any rsync. Exit non-zero on failure.
rule.rationale: A deploy that starts without the env file will sync code correctly but the service will fail to start with a config error.

rule.statement: Use `rsync -a --delete` with excludes for `.git/`, `__pycache__/`, `.venv/`, `.env`, and `node_modules/`. Source path must end with `/` to sync contents, not the directory itself.
rule.rationale: Without the trailing slash, rsync creates a nested directory (`src/nero-core/`) instead of syncing files directly into `src/`.

rule.statement: Create the venv once; reinstall deps only when `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml` changes. Use `--rebuild-venv` flag to force recreate after Python version upgrades.
rule.rationale: Running `pip install` on every deploy adds 10-30 seconds to each deploy and generates churn in pip's cache.

rule.statement: After restarting a systemd service, wait up to 30 seconds for it to become active. For HTTP services, also confirm the health endpoint returns 200.
rule.rationale: A restart command exits successfully even if the process crashes immediately after. Polling `is-active` and then hitting `/health` gives confidence that the new code is actually running.

section.name: deploy-hook-system
section.p: Hooks let each service extend the deploy lifecycle without modifying the central script. Create shell scripts in `deploy-hooks/` inside the workspace service directory.

rule.statement: Support hooks at: `pre-deploy`, `post-sync`, `pre-restart`, `post-restart`. The central deploy script checks for each file and sources it if present.
rule.rationale: Database migrations (`post-sync`), connection draining (`pre-restart`), and notification webhooks (`post-restart`) are service-specific concerns.

rule.statement: Define a deploy order array: SDK/libraries first, then infra (Docker), then application services, then frontend. When SDK changes, redeploy all dependents.
rule.rationale: A service that restarts before its shared library is updated will start against stale library code.

template.purpose: Full deploy orchestrator: service registry, pre-checks, rsync, venv, restart, health check
Git-based rollback: checkout previous commit, redeploy, print undo command
Standalone pre-deploy validation: env file, disk space, git status, backing services
Show all service status, Docker containers, health endpoints, resource usage
